history.notes: Audit completed. Root issue is a branch regression in sidebar height/overflow ownership after history bootstrap populates recentThreads; current branch diverged from main's restored shell-height contract.
Reverting sidebar shell height/overflow ownership to stable main contract while preserving ledger alignment fixes.
Bootstrap refreshed. Auditing chat workbench shell, session ledger activation path, message rendering, and CSS ownership before planning a production fix.

Implementing production historical session rendering fix: hydrate structured assistant artifacts from persisted session history, move loaded-session rendering onto canonical workbench markdown/artifact components, and keep CSS ownership in workbench-shell.css to avoid regression from globals.css.

Audit in progress. Branch/source-of-truth check, active /chat entrypoint inspection, and historical-session rendering ownership review across ChatWorkbenchV2, workbench-shell.css, and any legacy chat surfaces.

Audit in progress for main workbench shell. Verified /chat is already single-owner, AppDashboardShell owns sidebar and ledger, and current shell still mixes card-based transcript with partial review-mode CSS and global prose/rail selectors. Planning a Claude-like split-scroll workbench with separate sidebar/main scrolling and non-card assistant transcript.
Implementing single-owner Claude-inspired shell refactor: split-scroll sidebar, flat document transcript, restrained evidence rail.
Auditing center-pane scroll chain and transcript typography after split-scroll shell refactor. C1 non-causal UI fix; no C2/C3 changes.
C1 frontend shell audit: empty-state centering failure is caused by chat-message-scroll sizing to content (flex: 0 0 auto), preventing chat-empty-shell from filling the main pane.
Implementing compact minimal redesign for sidebar nav and command tray; removing instrument detail text.

benchmarkSnapshot.honestCapabilityStatement: WARNING: All benchmarks pass but compliance gates are not fully satisfied. Not consolidation-ready.
